option_line: bffi_apHorus14:"And it's almost certainly a fake."
original: And it's almost certainly a fake.

Menes looks shocked.

Horus gives you a look of his own. "Of course it is," he says. "And here I thought only a Luddie would be foolish enough to believe in this sort of thing."

"Cousin!" Menes exclaims, appalled.

"Do not 'cousin' me!" Horus shouts, now, after an icy restraint held for so long.

"Remember how I saved you last time? The promises you made? And here, you've wasted stars-know how many credits on more nonsense, a bomb waiting to explode under the gens!"

option_line: bffi_apHorus15:Keep your mouth shut, for now
original: Keep your mouth shut, for now

"What would Reynard Hannan do if he knew what you were up to here, do you think?"

Menes shakes his head, speechless.

"He would loose the Church on us! And their bloody-minded Pathers! Think, think for once, 'cousin'." Horus spits the word poisonously. "Think of anyone in the Sector aside from yourself. Think of your gens!"

"The Chu- Path?" Menes sputters pathetically. "If Captain $playerLastName said it's a fake, it shouldn't be-"

"Oh, so now it's a fake?" Horus cuts him off. "Enough! Here is what will happen."

"Captain $playerLastName and $playerHisOrHer pet Knight of Ludd shall take possession of the damnable trinket. Immediately. As a sign of good faith to our Luddic 'friends' in the Church of Galactic Redemption."

He gives you a look, "See that they appreciate what I've done for them."

Back to Menes. "From now on I am taking personal control of your accounts. No frivolities without approval from my office."

Menes begins to make a sound of objection but is cut off as Horus raises a single finger. A Reaper torpedo, armed and ready to launch into the unshielded breach.

"You and I," Horus says, "Shall continue our conversation in private." He doesn't turn to you again. "Captain, if you would kindly?"

Despite knowing what it is - or, rather, isn't - it feels almost like sacrilege to simply reach over to touch this object of such presumed expense and such evident disgrace. A vessel assigned such power and import, faith and violence, despite its reified deceit.

It is the glowing prize at the center of this temple, this shrine to the ego of Menes Yaribay bought with the lucre of the Kazeronian empire, fueled by faith and blood from over two hundred cycles since someone who took the name Ludd did something, probably, half-remembered then gilded in glorious myth on a planet named Gilead.
The quiet of the air circulation looms. Horus clears his throat.
